title: Five broken things
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: The console is empty and there is one red line. The script dies on line two and nothing below it runs.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error before it reached the end.
hint2.crashed: Look at the red line in the console. It points at the first broken thing — not the only one.
hint3.crashed: ReferenceError: Cannot access before initialization means a name is used earlier than the line that creates it. TypeError: Assignment to constant variable means a const is being overwritten.
hint4.crashed: Fix it, submit again, and you will see the next one. The errors come out one at a time.
why.erased-source: You removed the quotes from the price.
hint2.erased-source: The price arrives as text. The task is to convert it where you use it, not to rewrite where it comes from.
why.var-left: There is still a var somewhere.
hint2.var-left: Look at what the block in the middle does. It should not change anything outside itself.
why.typed-answer: You typed a number by hand instead of swapping.
hint2.typed-answer: The eight and the twelve appear only where the names are created.
hint2.no-cups: One of the two things involving cups is unnecessary. Which one?
hint3.no-cups: const creates a name and gives it a value at once. The second line tries to change it, and that is why it breaks. The value is already there.
hint2.total-glued: 20 and 3 added together make 23. If you get 203, the plus sign has joined a number with text.
hint3.total-glued: There is a second reason total is wrong. Look at the block above it — something is doing to it what it should not.
hint2.no-swap: Two names are not enough. One of the values has to be kept before it gets overwritten.
why.wrong-count: There are not four lines in the console.
hint2.wrong-count: Print cups, total, morning and evening. One line each.
why.lost-p: A line has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: This file is broken in five places. Each one comes from a lesson in this section.
blocks.1.text: Two of the five break loudly. The other three run and give the wrong answer — quietly, with nothing complaining.
blocks.2.text: What it is supposed to do
blocks.3.text: The code counts the cups and the price, swaps the two shifts, and prints four numbers.
blocks.4.items.1: total is 23 — twenty cups plus three lev
blocks.4.items.4: four lines in the console, one for each
blocks.6.text: Do not read it all at once and do not fix five things at the same time. The code breaks on the first error, and everything below it is unknown — it might be fine, it might not. There is no way to know until you get there.
blocks.7.text: So: fix one, submit, look at what comes out. Then the next one.
blocks.8.text: This is also how you work with real code. First make it run. Then make it right.
blocks.10.text: Those are the easy ones. The console gives you the name of the error and the line. Read the message — it almost always says exactly what happened.
blocks.11.text: The three quiet ones are the hard ones. There the code runs, the numbers come out, and they are simply not the right numbers. For those the only way is to work out what you expect and compare.
blocks.13.text: Fix the five things. Do not touch the quotes on the price, and write the numbers 8 and 12 only once.
blocks.14.text: If you get stuck — the hint ladder is under every failed check. It does not give you the code, but it says where to look.
